content: Hey, I'm trying to learn Japanese but I'm struggling with kanji. Any tips?
Kanji can definitely be challenging! Have you tried using spaced repetition systems like Anki? They're great for building retention over time.
I've heard of Anki but never used it. I'm more of a marathon runner, so I like physical flashcards I can carry while training.
That's an interesting approach! You could create physical flashcards and review them during your runs. What's your marathon training schedule like?
I'm training for the Amsterdam Marathon in October. I run about 60-70 km per week right now, so I have plenty of time to review cards.
That's impressive! Amsterdam has a great marathon. Do you have a specific Japanese learning goal in mind, or is it more of a general interest?
I'm actually going to Tokyo for a computational linguistics conference in November, so I want to be able to navigate and have basic conversations.
Perfect timing! Tokyo will be an amazing place to practice. Have you been to Japan before, or is this your first trip?
First time! I'm really excited about the food - I've been learning to make sushi at home with my vintage sushi knife collection.
That's fantastic preparation! Making sushi at home is such a great way to connect with the culture. Do you have a favorite type of sushi to make?
I love making nigiri, but my mechanical keyboard collection is actually taking over my workspace right now. I need to reorganize before my trip.
It sounds like you have quite a few passions! How do you balance your PhD work with all these interests?
It's a challenge, but I find that running helps me think through my computational linguistics research problems. Plus, I'm working on a project about Japanese language processing.
That's a perfect combination of interests! Your upcoming trip to Tokyo will probably give you great insights for your research too.
